$comment: Single source of truth for the cross-SDK E2E. Every runner (Python, TS) loads e2e/fixtures/catalog.json + e2e/fixtures/skills.json, registers all tools and skills, and asserts the behavior below. Because the Python and TS SDKs wrap the same Rust BM25 core, identical assertions here enforce cross-SDK parity: a divergence makes exactly one runner fail. Queries are written so each has an unambiguous top-1 match; assertions intentionally check top-1 / membership (not full ordering) to stay robust to score ties while still catching real regressions, packaging breaks, and binding-load failures. The skill* / capabilities blocks cover the skills surface added in 0.2.0: SkillCatalog ranking, get_skill_content, the unified search_capabilities surface, and the skill->tool cross-pollination (a matched skill's declared `tools` are pulled into the tools bucket at score 0, even when the query doesn't match them).

searches.query: read a file from local disk
list the entries of a directory
run a sql query against the postgres database

capabilitySearch.query: read a file from local disk

skillSearches.query: deploy the web service to production
stabilize flaky failing tests in continuous integration

capabilities.query: create a postgres index to speed up a slow query

capabilitiesCrossPollination.$comment: The matched skill (deploy-web-service) declares tools=[github__merge_pull_request]; the query shares no terms with that tool's description, so it can only enter the tools bucket via cross-pollination — asserted by its presence AND score 0.
capabilitiesCrossPollination.query: deploy the web service to production
